quotes.quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.
quotes.rationale: An LLM using a literature API discards ideas too similar to prior work, gating passage to the next stage.

quotes.quote: After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal. Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality. Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.
quotes.rationale: Experimental results are judged and routed into re-planning of the next experiment, i.e. search control over the iteration loop.

quotes.quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
quotes.rationale: Reviewer decisions benchmarked against human ground-truth accept/reject labels: a discrimination study on aggregate quality.

quotes.quote: Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.
quotes.rationale: Reported performance is qualified; ensembling gave no gain, supporting a mixed finding.

quotes.quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
quotes.rationale: Reviewer output is admitted into the archive, giving a memory-update path alongside terminal assessment.

quotes.quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
quotes.rationale: Documented limitation of the reviewer's evidence access.

quotes.quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
quotes.rationale: An interactive rebuttal capability is described as possible but not implemented.

diagnostics.consequence: Irreversible discarding of ideas rests on an unvalidated novelty gate; false rejection rates are unknown.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).

diagnostics.statement: The ideation novelty filter gates ideas out of the pipeline but no reliability study is reported for its novelty judgments; the reported benchmark applies only to the separate paper reviewer.

diagnostics.consequence: Quality assessments admitted to memory may be systematically wrong on figure-dependent or ambiguous claims.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.

diagnostics.statement: The automated reviewer, whose output is terminal and feeds the archive, cannot view figures and cannot query authors, so its evidence base is restricted to text.

diagnostics.consequence: Closed computational feedback drives search on self-generated notes whose correctness is never externally checked.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal. Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality. Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.

diagnostics.statement: The authors themselves disclaim the scientific content produced by the loop, indicating that no validator channel establishes empirical adequacy of outputs.

diagnostics.consequence: The presence or absence of human gates on archive admission and loop continuation cannot be assessed.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.

diagnostics.statement: Human authority over generation, execution, validation, memory, and policy is not reported anywhere in the packet.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

recommendations.proposed_action: Run a novelty-specific validation of the ideation filter against held-out prior-art labels, reporting false-discard and false-retain rates separately from the paper reviewer benchmark.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reported false-discard and false-retain rates against a stated baseline filter
success_check.evidence_needed: A labelled idea benchmark with per-decision comparison, reported for the filter channel alone
success_check.observable: Discard/retain decisions of the ideation filter versus expert novelty labels on a fixed idea set

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add figure-aware review inputs and an author-query step to the automated reviewer, and re-benchmark decisions to test whether the text-only restriction produces systematic error.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: No significant accuracy gap between the two paper subsets
success_check.evidence_needed: Stratified re-run of the OpenReview ground-truth comparison
success_check.observable: Reviewer accuracy on figure-dependent versus text-only papers before and after the change

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.

More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

recommendations.proposed_action: Document the human roles governing archive admission and loop continuation, or state explicitly that no human gate exists.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every role (generator, executor, validator, memory, policy) has a stated value including explicit absence
success_check.evidence_needed: System documentation naming who, if anyone, approves archive entries and loop continuation
success_check.observable: An explicit statement of human authority per role in the system description

memory_structure: archive of scientific findings holding completed ideas and reviewer feedback
experimental journal notes conditioned on results
policy_type: LLM-driven iterative experiment re-planning loop capped at five iterations
LLM literature-similarity gate over generated ideas
